text: Air pollution is responsible for a series of health problems and many deaths. Hence, it is necessary that prediction tools are available for the purpose of management of the air quality by the governmental agencies so that necessary measures can be taken to reduce undesirable emissions a few days in advance of the episodes of poor air quality. In recent years, Machine Learning (ML) and in particular Deep Learning (DL) have attracted huge interest in the air quality forecasting literature. This study provides a thorough review of air quality forecasting papers presented in the literature between 2011 and 2021. After careful investigation, a total of 155 papers have been selected based on search results in prominent scientific databases. A comprehensive summary and comparisons are made for these papers based on their location, parameters of the predicted variables, input variables, evaluation metrics and the type of ML methods employed.
